mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding real economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and supports higher equity prices over a one-month horizon. This relationship holds as a persistent trend, not requiring any threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical INDPRO increases lead positive SPX returns
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO change on forward 20-day returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window or average forward return is not positive when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:Historical rising unemployment periods precede market downturns
failure_condition: In triggered months, mean forward 60-day return is not negative or more than 50% of instances have positive returns over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When both policy rates are elevated and unemployment is rising, the joint condition creates a stagflationary environment that severely discounts equity cash flows. The interaction of high rates with rising joblessness amplifies the negative return signal beyond each series individually.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary conditions historically negative for equities
failure_condition: The interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (out-of-sample IC improvement less than 0.01) over individual series in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, falling housing starts signal that higher borrowing costs are curbing investment and raising recession risk. This negative demand impulse depresses equity returns over the subsequent month, but the relationship only holds in the tightening regime.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, HOUST declines do not produce mean negative 20-day forward returns significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 4% year-over-year, it signals overheating and imminent monetary tightening, leading to higher discount rates and lower equity valuations. The threshold activation isolates periods of high inflationary pressure that are negative for forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation periods correlate with negative real equity returns
failure_condition: In months triggered by CPI > 4%, mean forward 20-day return is not negative or more than 60% of instances have positive returns over a decade-long backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment and retail sales reinforces a strong consumption-led expansion, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns. The joint signal of robust labor and consumer spending is more powerful than either indicator alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows > 100K month-over-month and RSAFS grows > 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment and retail sales historically precede bull markets
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform the best single-series signal in rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of the federal funds rate persistently compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate applied to future cash flows. This inverse relationship operates as a continuous trend without requiring a specific threshold trigger.
evidence_refs: fact:Interest rates and equity valuations are fundamentally linked via discount rates
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS level shows a positive rank IC with forward 5-day returns over any 5-year rolling window, or the average return following high-rate periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (unemployment below 4%), rising retail sales indicate robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns. This positive signal is only reliable when the labor market is already tight; outside this regime the relationship weakens or inverts.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending strength is especially positive in tight labor markets
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4% and RSAFS rises, mean forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year window or the signal is weaker than in loose labor markets.
